summary:
The remaining matches are all in the §7/§8 explanatory text (describing the removal), not in the active design. All stale phrasing is gone.

I rewrote `design.md` to resolve all seven findings. The core correction was that the previous iteration invented its own R1–R6; I re-grounded the design against the real requirements artifact (R1–R18) found at `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/.../requirements.md` and cited it throughout. The resolution order in §3.1 was rebuilt into eight ordered branches: iteration is now suffixed only when `iteration > 1` (F1/R4); two active steps name both (F2/R5); three or more collapse to "`<Phase>: N steps are running`" naming none (F3/R6); a new "Failed (error stop)" branch uses failure wording and names the phase/step, detected via a `failed` phase — distinct from a new "Canceled/Paused" branch that maps the deliberate `aborted` cooperative-cancel state to "Canceled" (F4/R9, F5/R11); and an unconditional catch-all final branch makes `build` a total function so the summary is never blank for any current or future status (F6/R12). I removed the unrequested `as_of` timestamp entirely and recorded it as a conscious scope decision (F7). I verified against the actual Pipeline/Phase/StepRun enums, explicitly flagging that no `paused` status exists in the schema yet (only `aborted`→"Canceled" is reachable today), and added an F-to-fix traceability table in §8.
